title: KomorebiConfig
description: Configuration schema for the Komorebi Omoi Agentic AI Runtime.

authToken.description: Secure secret token used for CLI and internal authorization.

host.description: Listening host address for the orchestrator.

port.description: Port on which the gateway API listens.

type.description: Underlying message transport. 'embedded' uses an in-memory/IPC broker for minimal memory overhead.

url.description: Connection string for external brokers (e.g. redis://localhost:6379).

port.description: Port used if launching an embedded Redis or event bus server.

maxMessageSizeKb.description: Maximum size of payload allowed on the bus to prevent OOM.

telegram.description: Telegram bot settings. You can define a single shared bot routing to multiple agents, or separate bots per agent.

sharedToken.description: Bot token used for a single-bot deployment routing dynamically based on Chat ID / Command.

bots.description: Config bindings for multiple Telegram bots mapped to individual agents.

token.description: Telegram bot token issued by BotFather.

agentId.description: ID of the agent bound to this bot.

allowedUserIds.description: List of Telegram user IDs authorized to interact with this agent.

mcpServers.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server definitions, available to all agents.

command.description: Command to start the local MCP server (e.g., node, python3).

env.description: Environment variables required by the server.

url.description: SSE endpoint URL for remote MCP servers.

agents.description: List of active agent instances. Maximum of 10 recommended for Pi 5.

id.description: Unique identifier for the agent instance.

workspace.description: Absolute directory path where this agent's workspace files and caches are stored.

name.description: The exact model name to invoke.

apiKey.description: API key for the provider. Can be an environment variable reference like '${GEMINI_API_KEY}'.

toolPolicy.description: Execution permissions and restrictions for the agent's ReAct loop tools.

sandboxType.description: Sandbox framework used. 'bubblewrap' is recommended for Pi 5.

allowedTools.description: List of MCP tools the agent can execute. Use ['*'] for all.

networkAccess.description: Allows sandbox execution environment to access the external web.

readWritePaths.description: Additional host directories the agent is allowed to access.
